items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` **Service directory:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` **Triggered by:** PR #58585 by `@azure-sdk` ## What Changed The `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` package was released as `1.0.0-beta.1` with support for four `Microsoft.ServiceNetworking` resources: `TrafficController`, `TrafficControllerFrontend`, `TrafficControllerAssociation`, and `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`. After the release, a version bump commit (#58585) was pushed to increment the version to `1.0.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)`. ## Gaps Found - **Empty "Authenticate the Client" section** — `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` has an `### Authenticate the Client` heading with no content. Provisioning libraries work declaratively (no runtime authentication needed), but the section should still explain how the generated Bicep/ARM is deployed via `azd` and link to relevant docs. - **Empty "Next steps" section** — `## Next steps` heading exists but is completely blank. It should link to related packages (e.g., `Azure.Provisioning`, `Azure.Provisioning.Network`) and relevant documentation. - **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` not documented** — The CHANGELOG lists this resource as supported in `1.0.0-beta.1`, but the README Examples section only demonstrates `TrafficController`, `TrafficControllerFrontend`, and `TrafficControllerAssociation`. There is no example or even a mention of `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`. - **Key concepts section is generic** — It describes declarative provisioning in general but does not name or explain the specific resources this package provides.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` — **Edit** 1. **Fill in the "Authenticate the Client" section.** Replace the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section body with content similar to other `Azure.Provisioning.*` packages (e.g., `sdk/servicenetworking/README.md` or `sdk/appservice/Azure.Provisioning.AppService/README.md`). Example content: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` is a declarative provisioning library. It generates Bicep (or ARM) templates that are deployed to Azure using tools such as the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) No SDK authentication credentials are required at provisioning time. ``` 2. **Fill in the "Next steps" section.** Add links to related packages and documentation: ```markdown ## Next steps - Explore [Azure.Provisioning](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) for the core declarative infrastructure primitives. - Explore [Azure.Provisioning.Network](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/README.md) for virtual network resources often used alongside Traffic Controllers. - Learn about [Application Gateway for Containers]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` 3. **Expand the "Key concepts" section** to name the supported resources: ```markdown ## Key concepts This library supports the following `Microsoft.ServiceNetworking` resources: - **`TrafficController`** — Represents an Application Gateway for Containers instance. - **`TrafficControllerFrontend`** — A frontend (public IP / hostname) attached to a Traffic Controller. - **`TrafficControllerAssociation`** — Associates a subnet with a Traffic Controller. - **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`** — A security policy applied to an Application Gateway for Containers instance. Each resource can be added to an `Infrastructure` object and rendered into deployable Bicep using `azd` or the Azure Provisioning framework. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Add a new snippet demonstrating `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`. 1. **Write the sample code** in `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/tests/BasicServiceNetworkingTests.cs` (or a new `BasicServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicyTests.cs`): ```csharp internal static Trycep CreateSecurityPolicyTest() { return new Trycep().Define( ctx => { #region Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy Infrastructure infra = new(); TrafficController controller = new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01); infra.Add(controller); ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy securityPolicy = new(nameof(securityPolicy), ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01) { Parent = controller, }; infra.Add(securityPolicy); #endregion return infra; }); } ``` > **Note:** Verify the correct constructor and property signatures by inspecting the generated source files under `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/src/Generated/`. Adjust property names as needed to match what was actually generated. 2. **Add the snippet placeholder** to `README.md` in the Examples section, after the existing Traffic Controller example: ````markdown ### Create a Security Policy This example demonstrates how to attach an `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` to a Traffic Controller. ```C# Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy ``` ```` The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will fill in the code block from the `#region` in the test file. ### Step 3: Verify README structure Current structure assessment: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started / Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started / Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started / Authenticate the Client | ❌ **Empty** — needs content | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Present but generic — expand to list resources | | Examples | ⚠️ Missing `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ❌ **Empty** — needs links | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Reference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` for canonical formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 servicenetworking` — confirm README was updated with the new snippet 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 servicenetworking` — regenerate public API listing (no public API changes expected, but run to confirm) 5. `dotnet format sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [ServiceNetworking] Docs: Fill gaps in Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking README after initial release
